title: Lavender's Blue
content:
1.
    Lavender's blue, diddle, diddle!
    Lavender's green;
    When I am king, diddle, diddle!
    You shall be queen.

2.
    Call up your men, diddle, diddle!
    Set them to work;
    Some to the plough, diddle, diddle!
    Some to the cart.

3.
    Some to make hay, diddle, diddle!
    Some to cut corn;
    While you and I, diddle, diddle!
    Keep ourselves warm.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Crane, titled "Lavender's Blue", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
